executive_strategic_insight.core_theme: Pivoting from developer security to AI/agentic security — becoming the security layer for AI-generated code and autonomous coding agents
executive_strategic_insight.strategic_focus: Snyk is aggressively repositioning as the AI security company. Their 'AI Security Fabric' launch and Evo orchestration platform signal a platform play for securing agentic AI workflows. Research output on prompt injection, toxic skills, and supply chain attacks on AI agents (1,467 vulnerable skills, 76 malicious payloads identified) establishes thought leadership. Partnerships with Vercel, Cline, Tessl, and uv show ecosystem expansion into the AI developer toolchain.
executive_strategic_insight.ecosystem_momentum: Major new partnerships with Vercel (AI skill ecosystem), Tessl (agent skills registry), Cline (autonomous coding security), and uv (Python package manager). Integration ecosystem expanding specifically around AI/agentic developer tools rather than traditional DevOps.
executive_strategic_insight.persona_shift: Defining a new buyer: the 'AI Security Engineer'. Targeting developers and platform teams building with AI coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Cline). Shifting from traditional AppSec to securing AI-assisted development workflows.
executive_strategic_insight.pricing_model_shift: Free tier maintained at 200 SCA / 100 SAST / 300 IaC tests per month. New 'Ignite' tier at $1,260/year per developer bridges Team ($25/mo) and Enterprise. Ignite includes 10 DAST targets and advanced risk factors — signals move toward consolidated platform pricing. Enterprise adds FedRAMP, data residency (US/EU/AUS), and Snyk Broker.

engineering_capacity.velocity_assessment: Very high — launched AI Security Fabric, Evo platform, published 5+ major security research pieces, and announced 4 strategic partnerships in a single quarter

key_items: Supply chain attack vectors on AI agents

intelligence_signals.job_board_signals: Hiring broadly across teams globally with emphasis on Emerging Talent programs. Likely heavy ML/AI engineering hiring given product direction. SF Innovation Hub launch suggests West Coast engineering expansion.
intelligence_signals.target_users: AI Security Engineers (new role they're defining), developers using AI coding agents (Cursor, Claude Code, Cline), platform engineering teams managing AI-assisted development pipelines
intelligence_signals.monetization_approach: Four-tier model: Free (developer acquisition) → Team ($25/mo) → Ignite ($1,260/yr, mid-market with DAST) → Enterprise (custom, FedRAMP). Creating 'AI security' as a new budget category.
intelligence_signals.differentiators: First-mover in AI/agentic security with research credibility. AI Security Fabric as platform play. Deep integration with AI coding tools. Creating the 'AI Security Engineer' job category gives them narrative control.

gaps_exposed: Securin has no AI/agentic security offering — Snyk is defining this category
No security coverage for AI-generated code or AI coding agent workflows
No DAST capability in mid-tier pricing
Lack of security research output on emerging AI threats
areas_of_focus: Monitor AI security category — may become mandatory for enterprise security stacks
Add AI-generated code analysis to ASPM pipeline
Invest in thought leadership around AI security threats
Evaluate whether VI can extend to AI/ML supply chain risks

product_specific.ASPM: Snyk's AI Security Fabric extends into AI-generated code — a dimension Securin doesn't cover. Ignite tier with DAST at $1,260/yr undercuts traditional ASPM pricing.
product_specific.Core (VI): Snyk's ToxicSkills research and AI vulnerability database expansion threatens VI's comprehensiveness if AI vulnerabilities become a significant category.
product_specific.APT: Snyk's research on AI supply chain attacks is a new threat vector APT intelligence should track.
